title: ～てからでないと
titleEn: Indicates that something cannot be done before a certain action is completed; 'not until', 'only after'.
explanation: Indicates that something cannot be done before a certain action is completed; 'not until', 'only after'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～てからでないと grammar point is used to express that an action cannot be done before another action has been completed. It can be translated as 'not until' or 'only after' in English. It attaches to a verb in the て-form, plus からでないと.

title: ～てこそ
structure: Verb-て form + こそ / い-Adjective + くてこそ / な-Adjective + でこそ / Noun + でこそ
structureEn: Verb-て form + こそ / い-Adjective + くてこそ / な-Adjective + でこそ / Noun + でこそ
explanation: Emphasizes the condition; 'only when', 'precisely because'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～てこそ grammar point is used to emphasize that 'only when' or 'precisely because' the stated condition is met, a certain result or meaning arises. For nouns and adjectives, similar forms appear (e.g., Noun + でこそ, い-Adjective くてこそ, な-Adjective でこそ).

title: ～でしょうがない
titleEn: Expresses an intense emotional or physical state; 'extremely', 'can’t help but'.
structure: い-Adjective(く) + てしょうがない / な-Adjective + で(は)しょうがない / Verb-て form + しょうがない
structureEn: い-Adjective(く) + てしょうがない / な-Adjective + で(は)しょうがない / Verb-て form + しょうがない
explanation: Expresses an intense emotional or physical state; 'extremely', 'can’t help but'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The pattern ～(て)しょうがない (or 〜でしょうがない) indicates a condition or feeling so intense that one is almost 'overcome' by it. It’s often used with emotions (e.g. 退屈で(しょうがない), 嬉しくてしょうがない, 寒くてしょうがない).

title: ～でたまらない
titleEn: Express extremely strong feelings or sensations; 'can't stand', 'unbearably'.
structure: い-Adjective (く) + てたまらない / な-Adjective + でたまらない / Verb-て form + たまらない
structureEn: い-Adjective (く) + てたまらない / な-Adjective + でたまらない / Verb-て form + たまらない
explanation: Express extremely strong feelings or sensations; 'can't stand', 'unbearably'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～でたまらない grammar point is used to convey very strong emotion or sensation—so strong it’s almost unbearable. It’s common with expressions like 暑くてたまらない, うれしくてたまらない, etc.

title: ～でならない
titleEn: Indicates an uncontrollable emotion or feeling; 'can’t help but', 'overwhelmingly'.
structure: な-Adjective + でならない / い-Adjective (く) + てならない / Verb-て + ならない
structureEn: な-Adjective + でならない / い-Adjective (く) + てならない / Verb-て + ならない
explanation: Indicates an uncontrollable emotion or feeling; 'can’t help but', 'overwhelmingly'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～でならない grammar pattern expresses a very strong, often involuntary feeling—e.g., '不思議でならない' (it’s so mysterious I can’t help thinking about it), '残念でならない' (I’m so disappointed), '心配でならない' (I’m extremely worried).

title: ～でばかりはいられない
titleEn: Cannot afford to only do something; cannot always rely on it.
structure: Verb-て form + ばかりはいられない / Noun + ばかりはいられない (with appropriate particles)
structureEn: Verb-て form + ばかりはいられない / Noun + ばかりはいられない (with appropriate particles)
explanation: Cannot afford to only do something; cannot always rely on it. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～でばかりはいられない grammar point is used when one realizes they cannot keep doing only that one thing or cannot always rely on a certain situation. It implies the need to take other actions or responsibilities.

title: ～ではないか
titleEn: Used to show a strong assertion or emphasis on one’s opinion; 'isn't it?', 'aren't you?', 'right?'.
structure: Noun + ではないか / い-Adjective + ではないか / な-Adjective + ではないか / Verb-casual + ではないか
structureEn: Noun + ではないか / い-Adjective + ではないか / な-Adjective + ではないか / Verb-casual + ではないか
explanation: Used to show a strong assertion or emphasis on one’s opinion; 'isn't it?', 'aren't you?', 'right?'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～ではないか grammar point is used at the end of a sentence to make a rhetorical question or a strong, emphatic statement. It often translates as 'isn't it…?' 'aren't you…?' or 'wouldn’t you say…?'.

title: ～てはならない
structure: Verb-て form + はならない
structureEn: Verb-て form + はならない
explanation: Express prohibition; 'must not', 'cannot', 'should not'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～てはならない grammar point is used to express a strong prohibition. It is often more formal or strict and can be translated as 'must not', 'cannot', or 'should not.'
